  /* ─── step requirement chips ───
     Helps users quickly distinguish required selections from optional
     adjustments. Three chip variants:
       .step-required  — needed to proceed (red-ish dot)
       .step-optional  — can be skipped (subtle outline)
       .step-defaults  — has sensible defaults; tweak if you want (cyan)
     Rendered in the step header just below the title. Field-level
     `.required-mark` and `.optional-mark` chips follow the same color
     vocabulary so a user scanning a long form can see at a glance which
     fields demand input vs which have a working default. */
  .step-chip {
    display: inline-block; margin: 0 0 12px;
    padding: 3px 10px;
    font-family: var(--font-display); font-size: 0.62rem;
    letter-spacing: 0.14em; text-transform: uppercase; font-weight: 600;
  }
  .step-chip.step-required {
    color: #ff7a7a; border: 1px solid rgba(255,122,122,0.35);
    background: rgba(255,122,122,0.06);
  }
  .step-chip.step-required::before { content: '● '; color: #ff7a7a; }
  .step-chip.step-optional {
    color: var(--text-faint); border: 1px solid var(--glass-border);
    background: transparent;
  }
  .step-chip.step-optional::before { content: '○ '; }
  .step-chip.step-defaults {
    color: var(--neon-cyan); border: 1px solid rgba(0,212,230,0.3);
    background: rgba(0,212,230,0.04);
  }
  .step-chip.step-defaults::before { content: '✓ '; opacity: 0.7; }
  /* Inline chip used inside .field-label to mark a single field within a
     step as required/optional/preset. Smaller and tighter than the step-
     header chip — sits beside the field title, not as its own line. */
  .field-label .required-mark,
  .field-label .optional-mark,
  .field-label .preset-mark {
    display: inline-block; margin-left: 6px;
    padding: 1px 6px;
    font-family: var(--font-body); font-size: 0.58rem;
    letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: lowercase; font-weight: 500;
    vertical-align: 1px;
  }
  .field-label .required-mark { color: #ff8585; background: rgba(255,133,133,0.08); border: 1px solid rgba(255,133,133,0.3); }
  .field-label .optional-mark { color: var(--text-faint); border: 1px solid var(--glass-border); }
  .field-label .preset-mark { color: var(--neon-cyan); background: rgba(0,212,230,0.04); border: 1px solid rgba(0,212,230,0.25); }

  /* ============ BUTTONS ============ */
  /* Base button — `min-width: 0` lets flex/grid children shrink below their
     content width instead of overflowing; `overflow-wrap: anywhere` makes
     long labels wrap inside the button rather than spill out the side.
     Combined, these eliminate the "content too wide for fixed-width button"
     visual bug across mobile and narrow sidebars. */
  .btn {
    background: transparent; color: var(--accent-warm); border: 1.5px solid var(--accent-warm);
    padding: 13px 20px; font-size: 0.92rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.04em;
    cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.25s; font-family: var(--font-body);
    box-sizing: border-box; min-width: 0; overflow-wrap: anywhere;
  }
  .btn:hover { background: rgba(201,168,85,0.08); border-color: var(--neon-cyan); color: var(--neon-cyan); box-shadow: var(--glow-cyan); }
  .btn.full { width: 100%; }
  /* Icon variant — opt-in for single-glyph buttons (⚙, ×, ↻, etc.). Tight
     padding; never grows to fill flex/grid space. Stays sized to its content
     so a single ⚙ doesn't take a whole 200px column next to a real label. */
  .btn.icon {
    padding: 6px 10px;
    font-size: 0.95rem;
    flex: 0 0 auto;
    min-width: 32px;
    line-height: 1;
  }
  .btn.icon.sm { padding: 4px 8px; min-width: 28px; font-size: 0.82rem; }
  .btn.secondary { background: transparent; color: var(--text-dim); border: 1px solid var(--wood-light);}
  .btn.secondary:hover { border-color: var(--accent); color: var(--text); background: rgba(184,154,74,0.04); box-shadow: none; }
  .btn.warm { background: transparent; color: var(--accent-warm); border: 1.5px solid var(--accent-warm); }
  .btn.warm:hover { background: rgba(201,168,85,0.08); border-color: var(--neon-cyan); color: var(--neon-cyan); box-shadow: var(--glow-cyan); }
  .btn.green { background: transparent; color: var(--accent-green); border: 1.5px solid var(--accent-green); }
  .btn.green:hover { background: rgba(92,184,122,0.08); box-shadow: 0 0 10px rgba(92,184,122,0.25); filter: brightness(1.08); }
  /* Primary action button — visually distinct, used for the most-important
     action on a screen (Begin Session, etc.). Larger, brighter, with
     a soft glow that draws the eye. Styling separate from .btn.green
     so we can distinguish "the one button that matters most on this
     screen" from "any green-ish secondary button". */

  /* ============ GALLERY LIGHTBOX ============ */
  /* Fullscreen image viewer with wheel/pinch zoom + drag pan. Sits on top of

  /* When a 3D flip transition is running, the layer gets perspective so
     the rotateX / rotateY transforms on bg-img elements actually look
     like cards flipping rather than instant snaps. The class is added
     at the start of the flip transition and removed on cleanup; an
     interrupted flip (e.g. session end mid-transition) is handled by
     bgEngine.cleanupAfterStop() which strips this class defensively. */
  .bg-layer.bg-flipping { perspective: 1500px; }
  .bg-layer.bg-flipping .bg-img { transform-style: preserve-3d; }
  .bg-img { position: absolute; inset: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: cover; opacity: 0; transition: opacity 3s ease-in-out; will-change: opacity, transform, filter, clip-path; }
  .bg-img.active { opacity: var(--bg-img-opacity, 0.35); }
  /* V1.3 — Ken Burns slow zoom/pan on active background images. Subtle
     pan in a random direction across 60-90s; restarts when a new image
     swaps in via the bg engine. Disabled by prefers-reduced-motion.
     Skipped on .bg-video so videos don't double-animate. */
  /* Ken Burns slow zoom-and-pan on the active background image.
     Bug fix — previous values (1.02 → 1.06 scale, ≤1.4% translate,
     80s cycle) were so subtle the user reported "static until a
     transition". Bumped to ~1.0 → 1.10 scale and ≤4% translate over
     50s so the motion is actually perceptible. The .alternate
     direction with ease-in-out gives a slow inhale/exhale feel
     rather than a jarring loop. Disabled by prefers-reduced-motion.
     Skipped on .bg-video so videos don't double-animate. JS soft-
     motion (when enabled) overrides this via inline `animation: none`. */
  body.bg-active .bg-img.active {
    animation: bg-ken-burns 50s ease-in-out infinite alternate;
    transform-origin: center center;
  }
  @keyframes bg-ken-burns {
    0%   { transform: scale(1.00) translate(0%, 0%); }
    50%  { transform: scale(1.06) translate(-2.4%, 1.6%); }
    100% { transform: scale(1.10) translate(2.4%, -1.6%); }
  }
  @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
    body.bg-active .bg-img.active { animation: none; transform: scale(1.02); }
  }

  /* V1.4 — Phase color-temperature shift overlay. A single full-stage
     color wash with low opacity that interpolates across the session
     arc: cool blue at induction, warming amber at peak work, cool blue
     again at wake. Body class drives the active hue. Adds an emotional
     temperature layer that anchors the user to where they are in the
     arc without explicit progress UI. */
  .stage::after {
    content: ''; position: absolute; inset: 0;
    pointer-events: none; z-index: 7;
    background: transparent; opacity: 0;
    transition: background 6s ease-in-out, opacity 6s ease-in-out;
    mix-blend-mode: soft-light;
  }
  body.in-session.phase-temp-cool .stage::after {
    background: radial-gradient(ellipse at center, rgba(96,180,230,0.30), rgba(60,120,180,0.12) 60%, transparent 100%);
    opacity: 1;
  }
  body.in-session.phase-temp-warm .stage::after {
    background: radial-gradient(ellipse at center, rgba(220,170,90,0.32), rgba(180,120,60,0.14) 60%, transparent 100%);
    opacity: 1;
  }
  body.in-session.phase-temp-deep .stage::after {
    background: radial-gradient(ellipse at center, rgba(140,100,200,0.26), rgba(80,60,140,0.12) 60%, transparent 100%);
    opacity: 1;
  }
  @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
    .stage::after { transition: none; }
  }

    background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.4); border: 0.5px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.12);
    border-radius: 3px; color: var(--text-faint);
    backdrop-filter: blur(4px);
    letter-spacing: 0.04em;
  }
  .hud-phase-chip:empty { display: none; }
  .phase-indicator { font-size: 0.7rem; letter-spacing: 0.15em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--text-faint); margin-bottom: 20px; }
  .stage { position: relative; width: 100%; height: 360px; margin: 0 auto 30px;overflow: hidden; background: #000; transition: background 1s; }
  /* Background layer & stage are siblings at z-index 0; stage paints later in
     DOM. Original rule made stage 75% opaque black when a bg was active,
     which drowned out AI images (effective opacity dropped to ~9%). The
     visualizer canvas inside .stage retains its own appearance — we just
     make the stage's BACKGROUND transparent so the bg-img beneath can show.
     A tiny dim (15% black) keeps high-contrast visualizers readable without
     obscuring the image. */
  body.bg-active .stage { background: rgba(0,0,0,0.15); }
  body.bg-active .highway-canvas, body.bg-active .spiral-svg, body.bg-active .pulse-rings { opacity: 0.8; }
  .fixation-dot {
    position: absolute;
    top: var(--fixation-y, 38%);
    left: var(--fixation-x, 50%);
    width: 8px; height: 8px;
    background: var(--fixation); box-shadow: 0 0 24px rgba(212, 220, 232, 0.6);
    transform: translate(-50%, -50%); z-index: 10;
    cursor: default;
  }
  /* When the user has moved the dot off its default, double-click on it
     reverts to default. The cursor change hints that interaction exists. */
  .fixation-dot.repositioned { cursor: pointer; }
  /* In-session shift+click affordance — a short-lived ring pulse where
     the user clicks. Applied via .stage.fixation-relocate-hint for ~1.2s
     after a relocate. Read by humans as "yes, that registered." */
  .stage.fixation-relocate-hint::before {
    content: '';
    position: absolute;
    top: var(--fixation-y, 38%);
    left: var(--fixation-x, 50%);
    width: 32px; height: 32px;
    border: 1.5px solid rgba(212, 220, 232, 0.7);
    border-radius: 50%;
    transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
    pointer-events: none;
    z-index: 11;
    animation: fixation-relocate-ring 1.2s ease-out forwards;
  }
  @keyframes fixation-relocate-ring {
    0%   { width: 16px; height: 16px; opacity: 1; }
    100% { width: 80px; height: 80px; opacity: 0; }
  }
  .fixation-dot.pulse { animation: dot-pulse 8s ease-in-out infinite; }
  @keyframes dot-pulse {
    0%, 100% { transform: translate(-50%, -50%) scale(1); opacity: 1; }
    50%      { transform: translate(-50%, -50%) scale(1.6); opacity: 0.6; }
  }

  /* ============ PERSONAL SYMBOL OVERLAY (V1.1) ============
     Faded image of the user's crafted-suggestion symbol, layered over
     the visualizer during the work phase. Reinforces installation by
     pairing the visual anchor with the spoken suggestion. ~30% peak
     opacity so it sits behind the fixation dot, slow breath pulsing
     synced to the work-phase pacing. Removed via fade-out on phase exit. */
  .personal-symbol-overlay {
    position: absolute; top: 50%; left: 50%;
    width: 280px; height: 280px; max-width: 60vw; max-height: 60vh;
    transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
    pointer-events: none; z-index: 8;
    opacity: 0; transition: opacity 1.5s ease-out;
    filter: blur(0.5px);
    mix-blend-mode: screen;
  }
  .personal-symbol-overlay.visible {
    opacity: 0.32;
    animation: symbol-breath 7s ease-in-out infinite;
  }
  .personal-symbol-overlay img {
    width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: contain;
    border-radius: 50%;
  }
  @keyframes symbol-breath {
    0%, 100% { transform: translate(-50%, -50%) scale(0.95); opacity: 0.22; }
    50%      { transform: translate(-50%, -50%) scale(1.05); opacity: 0.40; }
  }
  /* Reduced motion — drop the breath animation but keep the fade-in. */
  @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
    .personal-symbol-overlay.visible { animation: none; opacity: 0.28; }
  }

  /* ============ TOAST NOTIFICATIONS ============ */
  /* z-index: 10000 sits above every other modal layer:
     - bg-fullscreen (120), bg-lightbox preview popup (110),
     - modals (100), safety-screen (2000),
     - rule overlay (9000), stats-modal-overlay (9999).
     Toasts are ephemeral and must remain visible regardless of which
     modal happens to be open underneath them. The two z:99999 elements
     (debug banner top-right, emergency-export failsafe bottom-right)
     deliberately outrank toasts: they're both bug/recovery indicators
     that need to be visible even if a toast is showing. The failsafe
     sits at bottom:60px so it doesn't overlap the toast stack at
     bottom:14px — both bottom-right but vertically offset. */
  #toastContainer {
    position: fixed; bottom: 14px; right: 14px; z-index: 10000;
    display: flex; flex-direction: column-reverse; gap: 8px;
    max-width: 340px; pointer-events: none;
  }
  .toast {
    display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 9px;
    background: #16161c;padding: 9px 12px;
    font-size: 0.78rem; line-height: 1.5; color: #e2e0d8;
    pointer-events: all; box-shadow: 0 4px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.5);
    opacity: 0; transform: translateX(12px);
    transition: opacity 0.2s, transform 0.2s;
  }
  .toast.show { opacity: 1; transform: translateX(0); }
  .toast-info    { border-left: 3px solid #5fa0dc; }
  .toast-success { border-left: 3px solid #7dc87a; }
  .toast-warn    { border-left: 3px solid #f0a04a; }
  .toast-error   { border-left: 3px solid #e05050; }
  .toast-icon { flex-shrink: 0; margin-top: 1px; font-size: 0.82rem; }
  .toast-info .toast-icon    { color: #5fa0dc; }
  .toast-success .toast-icon { color: #7dc87a; }
  .toast-warn .toast-icon    { color: #f0a04a; }
  .toast-error .toast-icon   { color: #e05050; }
  .toast-text { flex: 1; white-space: pre-wrap; word-break: break-word; }
  .toast-close {
    background: transparent; border: none; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.3);

  /* ═══ CHAT-STYLE CHECK-IN — Perchance-default grey palette ═══
     Opt-in via the .chat-style modifier on the wrapping section. Restyles
     the existing check-in DOM (avatar/question/textarea/send button) into
     a proper conversational interface — message-bubble for the assistant
     question, chat-input row with autogrow textarea + integrated send
     button. Background and borders use Perchance's neutral grey palette
     so it visually reads as a different surface than the rest of the
     app (which uses our cyan/amber accent palette).

     Drop-in: add `chat-style` class to .pre-checkin or .post-checkin.
     All the original IDs and event wiring continue to work — no JS
     restructuring needed beyond the Enter-to-send keydown handler. */
  .pre-checkin.chat-style,
  .post-checkin.chat-style {
    background: #2b2b2b;
    border: 1px solid #444;
    border-left-width: 1px;       /* override .post-checkin-elevated cyan stripe */
    border-radius: 6px;
    padding: 14px 14px 12px;
    color: #ddd;
  }

  /* Phase row action group — used in #phaseList row markup. flex-wrap
     lets each button size to its own content (no equal-width grid),
     so long-text buttons like "🔊 audition" + "✨ generate" + "remove"
     don't squeeze each other. Wraps cleanly to a second row when the
     parent shrinks (mobile, sidebar-open). justify-content:flex-end
     keeps the group right-anchored so the dragHandle + label still
     sit at the left of the row. Replaces an earlier grid-template
     approach that gave equal-width cells and clipped longer labels. */
  .phase-action-row {
    display: flex;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
    justify-content: flex-end;
    align-items: center;
    gap: 6px;
    margin-left: 8px;
    flex-shrink: 0; /* don't let the parent flex squash this group */
  }
  .phase-action-row .preview-btn {
    flex: 0 0 auto; /* size to content, never grow or shrink */
  }

  /* ============ TRANSCRIPT PANEL ============ */

  /* When transcript is open, hide HUD elements that would otherwise float
     on top of the transcript body. text-display keeps updating with the
     current sentence even while transcript is open — letting it overlay
     scrolled transcript text creates a visually confusing double-layer
     of words. Phase-dots/indicator are above the transcript area so they
     stay; session-time and audio-indicator are inside the stage so they
     end up under the transcript naturally. */
  body.in-session:has(.transcript-panel.visible) .text-display { opacity: 0; pointer-events: none; }
  .transcript-panel .t-phase {
    color: var(--accent); font-size: 0.68rem;
    letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase;
    margin: 16px 0 6px; font-weight: 500;
  }
  /* First phase header shouldn't push the transcript content down — the
     panel already has 20px of top padding. */
  .transcript-panel .t-phase:first-child { margin-top: 0; }

     and immediately see where playback currently is. JS adds .t-active to
     both the header and the body div for the live phase. */

  /* ═══ BG soft-motion row — toggle + strength ═══
     Sits below the transition grid; applies a slow drift/zoom to the
     live BG between transitions so the image feels alive instead of
     frozen. Especially useful for hypnosis sessions where a static
     ambient image starts to read as "stuck" after 30+ seconds. */
  .bg-soft-motion-row {
    display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px;
    margin-top: 10px; flex-wrap: wrap;
  }
  .bg-soft-motion-row label.cb-label {
    display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px;
    cursor: pointer; flex: 1; min-width: 220px;
    font-size: 0.78rem; color: var(--text-dim);
    user-select: none;
  }

  /* Mobile sidebar drawer.

  /* Global button styling — forces a consistent amber-trim outlined look on
     primary action buttons. Buttons that intentionally have their own
     visual language are excluded by class:
       - .icon-btn, .filter-tab, .toggle-btn — existing exclusions
       - [class*="close"]              — close-X buttons keep their compact style
       - [class*="bg-preview"]         — image preview popup buttons (action row + nav)
       - [class*="bg-fs"]              — fullscreen viewer buttons (prev/next/zoom)
       - [class*="bg-gallery-action"]  — inline gallery toolbar action chips
       - .step-chip                    — step requirement chips
       - .phase-reimagine              — Custom-block ✨ rewrite button (cyan accent)
     Without these exclusions the dark-backdrop circular arrows and floating
     overlays in the image flow render as amber-uppercase rectangles. */
  .btn, button, .preview-btn, .filter-tab {
    transition: background 0.12s, color 0.12s, border-color 0.12s, box-shadow 0.12s !important;
  }

    color: var(--accent-warm) !important;
    border: 1.5px solid var(--accent-warm) !important;
    box-shadow: none !important;

    letter-spacing: 0.05em;

     Force them into col 2 so they flow under the title text at full width. */
  .config-toggle-grid .toggle-row label .hint { grid-column: 2; }

  /* Consent modal must render on top of the Options modal that contains its
     triggering checkbox. Both modals share z-index: 100 by default and DOM order
     would otherwise put Options on top. */
  #remoteConsentModal { z-index: 200; }

  /* ─── utility classes — extracted from repeated inline styles ─── */
  /* Compact <input type="number"> used in dense settings panels (Safety
     limits, playback dynamics, etc.). The input element gets a chunky
     1rem font-size from the global iOS-zoom guard at line 2363; this
     class wins specificity (class > element) so the inputs render at
     0.74rem to fit the row layout. */
  .compact-num-input { font-size: 0.74rem !important; padding: 2px 6px; }
  /* Suffix label after a tight input — ×, %, s, min, etc. */
  .unit-suffix { color: var(--text-faint); font-size: 0.66rem; }
  /* Tertiary hint text inside a primary .hint span (parenthetical clarifier). */
  .hint-sub { font-weight: normal; font-size: 0.66rem; }
  /* Right-aligned numeric badge that mirrors the value of a sibling slider.
     The 32px min-width covers both short ("35") and longer ("1.0×") values
     so all rows align consistently. */
  .slider-val { min-width: 32px; text-align: right; }
  /* Glass-bg panel used for Options-modal sub-sections (Safety limits,
     Playback dynamics, Session mode, etc.). */
  .panel-card {
    margin-top: 14px;
    padding: 10px 12px;
    background: var(--glass);
    border: 1px solid var(--glass-border);
  }
  /* Section header for sub-panels — sits in a flex row with action button. */
  .panel-section-title { font-size: 0.82rem; color: var(--text); }
  /* Round 26 — speed preset chips for the haptic live-control panel.
     Small monospace pills under the speed slider, matching the existing
     dim-foreground / accent-on-active vocabulary used elsewhere. */
  .speed-preset-chip {
    background: transparent;
    color: var(--text-faint);
    border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.10);
    padding: 2px 8px;
    font-family: monospace;
    font-size: 0.62rem;
    cursor: pointer;
    transition: color 0.15s, border-color 0.15s, background 0.15s;
    border-radius: 2px;
  }
  .speed-preset-chip:hover {
    color: var(--text-dim);
    border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.25);
  }
  .speed-preset-chip.active {
    color: var(--neon-cyan);
    border-color: rgba(0,212,230,0.5);
    background: rgba(0,212,230,0.06);
  }
  /* Round 27 — pattern editor tool toggle pills. Same visual vocabulary
     as speed-preset-chip but slightly larger for click-target comfort. */
  .pe-tool-btn {
    background: transparent;
    color: var(--text-faint);
    border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.10);
    padding: 3px 9px;
    font-family: monospace;
    font-size: 0.66rem;
    cursor: pointer;
    transition: color 0.15s, border-color 0.15s, background 0.15s;

  }
  .pe-tool-btn:hover {

  .cstp-myths { margin: 8px 0; }

          <!-- ── Hypnotic Susceptibility (P1.1) ───────────────────────
               Self-administered brief inspired by the Stanford HIP. ~3
               minutes to complete; produces a 0-10 score that biases
               method recommendations (low → permissive Ericksonian,
               high → direct/rapid acceptable). Stored in pfSusceptibility
               (hidden input, populated by the modal). -->
          <details class="pf-extended" style="margin-top:6px">

            <div class="sublabel" style="font-size:0.58rem;margin-bottom:8px;color:var(--text-faint)">A short self-administered brief (~3 minutes) producing a 0–10 score. Most adults sit between 4.5 and 7 breaths per minute. The RF-finder runs a quick 5-minute sweep to find yours.</div>

          <!-- ── Roadmap #13 — Trance anchor (classical conditioning) ──
               A unique sound paired with the work-phase peak across many
               sessions builds a conditioned cue — the user can fire it
               during the day to recall a fragment of the settled state. -->
          <details class="pf-extended" style="margin-top:6px" id="anchorDetails">
            <summary style="font-size:0.7rem;font-weight:600;color:var(--text);cursor:pointer;padding:6px 0">🔔 Trance anchor</summary>
            <div class="sublabel" style="font-size:0.58rem;margin-bottom:8px;color:var(--text-faint)">A small sound played once per session at peak depth. Over 10–20 sessions it becomes a Pavlovian cue — tap the daytime button anywhere and a fragment of the settled state returns. Use a sound you don't hear elsewhere in your day.</div>
            <div class="pf-row"><label class="pf-label">Anchor sound</label>
              <select id="pfAnchorSound" style="width:100%;padding:6px;font-size:0.74rem">
                <option value="off">Off — no anchor</option>
                <option value="bell-c5">Bell (C5 — bright clear)</option>
                <option value="bowl-low">Bowl (low — warm sustain)</option>
                <option value="om-tone">Om tone (132 Hz drone)</option>
                <option value="rim-tap">Rim tap (single soft strike)</option>
                <option value="tine">Music-box tine (fragile)</option>
              </select>
            </div>
            <div style="display:flex;gap:8px;margin-top:8px">
              <button class="btn secondary" id="anchorPreviewBtn" type="button" style="flex:1;font-size:0.7rem;padding:6px">▶ preview</button>
              <button class="btn primary"   id="anchorTapBtn"     type="button" style="flex:1;font-size:0.7rem;padding:6px">🔔 fire anchor now</button>
            </div>
            <div id="anchorTapCount" style="font-size:0.62rem;color:var(--text-faint);text-align:center;margin-top:6px"></div>
          </details>

            <div class="sublabel" style="font-size:0.58rem;margin-bottom:8px;color:var(--text-faint)">Two short additions to the moment between "begin" and the actual session: a 3-question priming row (the act of answering primes the response — Kirsch 1985), and a 60-second imagery warm-up that builds absorption over time. Both opt-out.</div>

          <!-- ── Roadmap #16 — Soundscape spatializer ──────────────────
               Routes the soundscape through a Web Audio HRTF panner so
               the ambient orbits around the listener (or stays at a
               fixed compass position). Provides an exogenous spatial
               scene for users who can't easily generate one mentally.
               Wants headphones for full effect. -->
          <details class="pf-extended" style="margin-top:6px" id="spatialDetails">
            <summary style="font-size:0.7rem;font-weight:600;color:var(--text);cursor:pointer;padding:6px 0">🎧 Spatial soundscape</summary>
            <div class="sublabel" style="font-size:0.58rem;margin-bottom:8px;color:var(--text-faint)">Routes the soundscape through a 3D audio panner so the ambient orbits around you (or sits at a fixed direction). Best with headphones — speakers reduce to a regular stereo pan. Provides a spatial scene without requiring you to imagine one.</div>

            <div class="pf-row" style="margin-top:6px">
              <label class="pf-label" style="font-size:0.7rem">Mode</label>
              <select id="pfSpatialMode" style="width:100%;padding:6px;font-size:0.74rem">
                <option value="off" selected>Off — flat stereo</option>
                <option value="orbit-slow">Slow orbit (1 rev / 5 min)</option>
                <option value="orbit-medium">Medium orbit (1 rev / 3 min)</option>
                <option value="orbit-fast">Fast orbit (1 rev / 1 min — drift effect)</option>
                <option value="fixed-front">Fixed — directly ahead</option>
                <option value="fixed-behind">Fixed — directly behind</option>
                <option value="fixed-above">Fixed — overhead (raised)</option>
              </select>
            </div>
          </details>

          <!-- ── Roadmap #21 — IFS skeptic-friendly framing ────────────

The script will explicitly speak to the part of you that's skeptical, analytical, tired, or resistant — inviting it to stay alert while the curious part does the practice. Composes with any persona.</div>
            <select id="pfIfsMode" style="width:100%;padding:6px;font-size:0.74rem">
              <option value="off" selected>Off — standard framing</option>

              <option value="parts">Parts-aware (IFS) — multiple parts, each addressed</option>
              <option value="felt-sense">Felt-sense (Gendlin) — body-first, "where does this question live?"</option>
            </select>
          </details>

    <!-- STEP 1: GUIDE -->

        <!-- Biometric sensor bridge (Round 25, Pass 9; HR-OBS native protocol added Round 29).
             Same per-session opt-in pattern; loopback WebSocket to a local adapter that
             pushes biometric signals into the engagement engine. Two protocols are
             auto-detected on the same socket:
               • Generic HNE format: {"signal":"heartRate","value":0.5,"weight":0.4}
               • HR-OBS Monitor v2: {"type":"heart_rate","data":{"bpm":72}}  ← translated
                 internally to normalized heartRate + derived relaxation (drop from
                 personal baseline). Lets users plug in the open-source Heart Rate
                 OBS Monitor (Samsung / Wear OS / Mi Band / Fitbit via Gadgetbridge)
                 with zero glue code. -->
        <label class="toggle-row toggle-row-cb" for="sensorBridgeOptInThisSession" style="align-items:flex-start;padding:6px 0;margin-top:4px">
          <input type="checkbox" id="sensorBridgeOptInThisSession">
          <span>
            📈 Biometric sensor bridge <span style="color:var(--text-faint);font-weight:400">— heart rate, HRV, breath, EEG</span>
            <span class="hint" style="line-height:1.5">
              Connects to a local adapter pushing biometric data over WebSocket. Two protocols auto-detected:
              <br>• <strong>Generic HNE</strong> (default <code style="font-size:0.65rem">ws://localhost:8765</code>): <code style="font-size:0.65rem">\{"signal":"heartRate","value":0.5,"weight":0.4\}</code>. Signals: heartRate, hrv, gsr, breath, eegFocus, custom1-3.
              <br>• <strong>Heart Rate OBS Monitor v2</strong> (<code style="font-size:0.65rem">ws://localhost:8080</code>): supports Samsung Galaxy Fit/Watch, Mi Band, Fitbit via Gadgetbridge — sends raw BPM, HNE auto-normalizes and derives a <em>relaxation</em> signal (drop from personal baseline). No glue code needed.
              <br>Each feeds the engagement engine and is exposed to rules as <code style="font-size:0.65rem">heartRate</code>, <code style="font-size:0.65rem">relaxation</code>, <code style="font-size:0.65rem">heartRateBpm</code>.
            </span>
          </span>
        </label>
        <div id="sensorBridgeStatusRow" style="display:none;font-size:0.66rem;color:var(--text-faint);margin-top:6px;padding-left:24px">

          <!-- Round 26 — Funscript heatmap. Color-coded intensity bar
               showing the loaded track's energy distribution at a

               fast. Standard funscript-utils convention used by

               from the setOnTrackChange handler. The playhead overlay
               (a thin vertical line) ticks during session playback. -->

          <!-- Round 27 — Secondary tracks. The main funscript drives the
               linear (stroke) axis. Optional secondary tracks can drive
               the vibrate, rotate, or oscillate axes simultaneously, all
               sharing the main playhead. Useful when you want a stroke
               pattern PLUS a vibration pattern at different rates. The
               panel is hidden until at least one secondary is loaded;
               the "+ secondary" button is the entry point. -->

            <summary style="font-size:0.66rem;color:var(--text-dim);cursor:pointer">▸ secondary axes <span id="funscriptSecondariesCount" style="color:var(--text-faint)">— none</span></summary>
            <div class="hint" style="font-size:0.62rem;line-height:1.45;margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:8px">
              Add a track on a non-stroke axis (vibrate / rotate / oscillate). Secondary tracks share the main playhead — they start, stop, pause, and seek with the primary track. Useful for layered haptics: stroke from one curve, vibration intensity from another. Each axis can hold one secondary track; loading a new one to that axis replaces the previous.
            </div>
            <div id="funscriptSecondariesList" style="font-size:0.66rem;margin-bottom:6px"></div>
            <div style="display:flex;align-items:center;gap:6px;flex-wrap:wrap">
              <select id="funscriptSecondaryAxis" style="padding:3px 6px;background:var(--bg-deep);border:1px solid var(--glass-border);color:var(--text);font-size:0.66rem">
                <option value="vibrate">vibrate</option>
                <option value="rotate">rotate</option>

              </select>
              <button type="button" id="funscriptSecondaryLoadBtn" class="btn secondary" style="padding:2px 10px;font-size:0.62rem">+ add</button>

              <button type="button" id="funscriptSecondaryClearAllBtn" class="btn secondary" style="padding:2px 10px;font-size:0.62rem">clear all</button>
            </div>
          </details>
          <details id="liveControlDetails" style="margin-top:10px;padding:6px 8px;background:rgba(255,255,255,0.025);border:0.5px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08);border-radius:4px">

               below with distinct accent styling so destructive + history
               actions don't visually mingle with the additive ones. -->
          <div class="sublabel" style="margin-top:14px;margin-bottom:6px;font-size:0.7rem;color:var(--text-faint);text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.06em">Add script block</div>
          <div class="preview-row">
            <button class="preview-btn" id="addIntroductionPhaseBtn" title="Adds an Introduction — brief opener where the guide introduces themselves and names the session theme. Sits at the very front, before settling.">introduction</button>
            <button class="preview-btn" id="addPermissionPhaseBtn" title="Adds a Permission block — asks the user to consent to the work, out loud, with a head nod, or silently inside. Sits after introduction, before settling.">permission</button>
            <button class="preview-btn" id="addInductionPhaseBtn" title="Adds an Induction — auto-adds Wake + Reintroduction if missing">induction</button>
            <button class="preview-btn" id="addDeepenerPhaseBtn" title="Adds a Deepener — auto-adds a paired Lightener. Stackable for nested deepening.">deepener (nest)</button>
            <button class="preview-btn" id="addLevelPhaseBtn" title="Adds a Level — sustained-depth tonic between two deepeners. Holds the user at the current depth before going deeper.">level</button>
            <button class="preview-btn" id="addWorkPhaseBtn">work block</button>
            <button class="preview-btn" id="addAffirmPhaseBtn">affirmation</button>
            <button class="preview-btn" id="addPraisePhaseBtn" title="Adds a Praise block — guide notices and validates something specific the user just did. Sits after work / affirm, before lightener.">praise</button>
            <button class="preview-btn" id="addRewardPhaseBtn" title="Adds a Reward — felt-sense payoff for the work just done (a small ease, a quiet warmth). Body-level, present-moment. Sits after praise / work.">reward</button>
            <button class="preview-btn" id="addTrainingPhaseBtn" title="Adds a Training — skill rehearsal in trance. Lead the user through 3-5 repetitions of one specific practice (mental, sensory, or somatic). Each rep slightly easier than the last.">training</button>
            <button class="preview-btn" id="addTeachingPhaseBtn" title="Adds a Teaching — psychoeducation primer in plain language. Explains the method, normalizes the experience, sets expectations. Best slotted between settling and induction.">teaching</button>
            <button class="preview-btn" id="addGoalPhaseBtn" title="Adds a Goal — outcome anchor / future-pacing. Vivid sensory rehearsal of what success FEELS like (somatic), not a goal-setting exercise (cognitive).">goal</button>
            <button class="preview-btn" id="addMissionPhaseBtn" title="Adds a Mission — concrete posthypnotic homework. ONE small doable task tied to waking life. Delivered as the user is surfacing.">mission</button>
            <button class="preview-btn" id="addSoundingPhaseBtn" title="Adds a Sounding — a brief meta-awareness pause where the user notices their current internal state without changing it.">sounding</button>
            <button class="preview-btn" id="addCustomPhaseBtn" title="Adds a user-authored block. Edit the text directly, or use Reimagine to have your guide rewrite it.">custom</button>
            <button class="preview-btn" id="addLightenerPhaseBtn">lightener</button>
            <button class="preview-btn" id="addWakePhaseBtn">wake</button>
            <button class="preview-btn" id="addReintroPhaseBtn">reintro</button>
          </div>
          <div class="sublabel" style="margin-top:14px;margin-bottom:6px;font-size:0.7rem;color:var(--text-faint);text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.06em">History &amp; reset</div>
          <div class="preview-row preview-row-warn">
            <button class="preview-btn preview-btn-warn" id="normalizePhaseOrderBtn">↺ reset to default order</button>
            <button class="preview-btn preview-btn-warn" id="undoBtn" disabled title="Undo (Ctrl+Z / Cmd+Z)">↶ undo</button>
            <button class="preview-btn preview-btn-warn" id="redoBtn" disabled title="Redo (Ctrl+Shift+Z / Cmd+Shift+Z)">↷ redo</button>
          </div>
          <!-- Reimagine mode toggle — used by Custom block ✨ buttons -->
          <div id="reimagineModeRow" style="margin-top:10px; padding:8px 10px; background:var(--bg-soft); border:1px solid var(--glass-border); display:none">
            <div style="font-size:0.7rem; color:var(--text-faint); letter-spacing:0.06em; text-transform:uppercase; margin-bottom:4px">reimagine mode</div>
            <div style="display:flex; gap:6px; flex-wrap:wrap; align-items:center">
              <button type="button" class="preview-btn reimagine-mode-btn" data-mode="guide" title="Rewrites in the persona's voice only. No profile data injection.">⚡ guide voice only</button>
              <button type="button" class="preview-btn reimagine-mode-btn" data-mode="smart" title="Rewrites in the persona's voice with Smart Director profile + memory context.">✦ smart director</button>
              <span id="reimagineModeHint" style="font-size:0.7rem; color:var(--text-dim); margin-left:6px"></span>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="sublabel" id="phaseStructureHint" style="margin-top:8px; font-size:0.72rem; color:var(--text-faint)">
            Default structure: <span style="color:var(--text-dim)">\{\{Settling\}\} \{\{Induction\}\} \{\{Deepener\}\} &rarr; (\{\{Work\}\}, \{\{Affirmation\}\}) &times; N &rarr; \{\{Lightener\}\} \{\{Wake\}\} \{\{Reintroduction\}\}</span>.
            Deepener / Lightener pairs can be nested for progressive deepening.
          </div>
          <div class="sublabel" id="tokensLegend" style="margin-top:6px; font-size:0.7rem; color:var(--text-faint)">
            <strong style="color:var(--text-dim)">Script tokens</strong> (resolved at generation &amp; playback):
            <span style="color:var(--text-dim)">\{\{user\}\}</span> first name or &ldquo;you&rdquo; &middot;
            <span style="color:var(--text-dim)">\{\{nar\}\}</span> persona name &middot;
            <span style="color:var(--text-dim)">\{\{intention\}\}</span> your intention &middot;
            <span style="color:var(--text-dim)">\{\{affirmation\}\}</span> a random affirmation
          </div>
        </div>
        <!-- Session diagnostics — pre-session analysis surfaces structural issues -->
        <div class="detail-panel" id="sessionDiagnosticsPanel" style="margin-top:12px">
          <div class="detail-title">session diagnostics</div>
          <div id="sessionDiagnosticsList"></div>
        </div>
      </div>

      <div class="pre-checkin chat-style" id="preMoodSection">
        <div class="checkin-header">
          <div class="checkin-avatar">💬</div>
          <div class="checkin-question">how are you arriving?</div>
          <button type="button" id="preCheckinTextToggle" class="checkin-mode-toggle">use sliders instead</button>
        </div>
        <!-- Conversational input — the default. Free text parsed via
             keyword lexicon (no AI call) into mood/tension/energy. Auto-
             applies on blur so users who skip the send button still get
             their signal captured. The round in-wrap send button is the
             chat-style version; the old #preCheckinTextApply below it
             stays in the DOM for non-chat-style fallback (hidden via CSS).
             Both call _applyPreCheckinText() through their click wires. -->
        <div id="preCheckinTextWrap" class="checkin-text-wrap">
          <textarea id="preCheckinText" rows="1" maxlength="200" placeholder="say a few words — e.g. tired but mind is racing, feeling tense after a long day, calm but a little foggy"></textarea>
          <button type="button" id="preCheckinTextSendCircle" class="checkin-send-btn" disabled aria-label="send" title="send (Enter)">↑</button>
        </div>
        <div class="checkin-text-actions">
          <span id="preCheckinTextHint" class="checkin-hint">we'll read mood, tension, and energy from what you write</span>
          <button type="button" id="preCheckinTextApply" class="checkin-send-btn">send →</button>
        </div>
        <!-- Sliders — hidden by default, revealed via toggle. The
             textarea parser pushes values here so they always reflect
             the canonical state regardless of which input mode the
             user is in. -->
        <div id="preCheckinSliders" class="pre-checkin-grid" style="display:none">
          <div class="pre-checkin-item">
            <div class="pre-checkin-label">mood</div>
            <div class="pre-emoji-row" id="preMoodEmojis">
              <button class="mood-emoji" data-val="1" title="very low">😞</button>

              <button class="mood-emoji" data-val="9" title="great">😊</button>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="pre-checkin-item">

            <input type="range" id="preTensionSlider" min="0" max="10" value="5">
            <div class="post-checkin-ends"><span>relaxed</span><span>very tense</span></div>
          </div>
          <div class="pre-checkin-item">
            <div class="pre-checkin-label">energy <span class="pre-val" id="preEnergyVal">5</span></div>
            <input type="range" id="preEnergySlider" min="0" max="10" value="5">
            <div class="post-checkin-ends"><span>exhausted</span><span>energized</span></div>
          </div>
        </div>
        <!-- Readiness score — derived from the three signals above. Live-updated.
             Inverts tension so high tension subtracts; mood + energy add. -->
        <div id="readinessRow" style="margin-top:10px;padding:8px 12px;background:var(--glass);border:1px solid var(--glass-border);display:flex;align-items:center;gap:10px;flex-wrap:wrap">
          <span style="font-size:0.62rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.1em;color:var(--text-faint)">readiness</span>

        </div>
      </div>

      <div class="disclaimer">
        <strong>Reminder:</strong> Therapeutic methods feel like nothing for the first 5–8 minutes — that's the mechanism. Demonstration methods should produce noticeable effects. Don't drive after. Skip if you have epilepsy, dissociative disorders, or psychosis.

              are providing data)

         rest of the HUD. Re-renders on a 1s interval during playback;
         tear-down lives in the same code path that ends a session. -->

  <!-- ============ SOFT-PAUSE OVERLAY ============
       First Esc press during a session shows this. Pauses speech + drone,
       gives the user a breath, and offers two clear paths: continue, or
       commit to a full counted re-alert end. Second Esc (while open) commits.
       Layer 50 (above session, below full modals at 100). -->
  <div class="soft-pause-overlay" id="softPauseOverlay" role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby="spTitle">
    <div class="soft-pause-card">
      <h2 id="spTitle">Paused — you're safe</h2>
      <p class="sp-line">Take a breath. Feel your feet on the ground.</p>
      <p class="sp-sub">Nothing is happening. The session is on hold. You can continue when you're ready, or end with a full counted re-alert.</p>
      <div class="sp-time" id="spTimeNow"></div>
      <div class="sp-actions">
        <button type="button" class="sp-continue" id="spContinueBtn">▸ continue session</button>
        <button type="button" class="sp-end" id="spEndBtn">end now &amp; re-alert</button>
      </div>
      <div class="sp-hint">Press <kbd>Esc</kbd> again to end now · <kbd>Space</kbd> or <kbd>Enter</kbd> to continue</div>
    </div>

<!-- AUTO-TEMPO CLEANUP OPTIONS MODAL -->
<div class="modal-backdrop" id="autoTempoOptsModal">
  <div class="modal" style="max-width:560px">
    <h2>auto-tempo cleanup options</h2>
    <div style="font-size:0.78rem;color:var(--text-dim);line-height:1.55;margin-bottom:12px">
      Choose which characters should be removed from the script when auto-tempo runs. Characters that aren't in this set are left alone. The PAUSE markers (in square brackets, e.g. PAUSE 3) and <code>\{\{tokens\}\}</code> are always preserved regardless.
    </div>
    <label class="toggle-row" for="autoTempoStripOrphan" style="align-items:flex-start;margin-bottom:14px">
      <input type="checkbox" id="autoTempoStripOrphan">
      <span>
        Strip orphan punctuation (recommended)
        <span class="hint" style="line-height:1.5">Removes leftover <code>" . "</code>, <code>" , "</code>, ellipsis (<code>"…"</code> / <code>"..."</code>) and similar floating punctuation patterns that the narrator otherwise reads aloud as "dot" or "comma" or "ellipsis". These are usually leftovers from stripped square-bracket annotations like a stage-direction or topic-sentence header that the AI sometimes generates.</span>
      </span>
    </label>
    <label class="toggle-row" for="autoTempoCollapseSpaces" style="align-items:flex-start;margin-bottom:14px">
      <input type="checkbox" id="autoTempoCollapseSpaces">
      <span>
        Collapse multi-space gaps (recommended)
        <span class="hint" style="line-height:1.5">Stripping a character that sat between two spaces (like <code>"a * b"</code> → <code>"a&nbsp;&nbsp;b"</code>) leaves multi-space clusters that confuse TTS pacing — the narrator either swallows the gap or stutters. Turn off only if you're feeding the script through another preserving-whitespace tool first.</span>
      </span>
    </label>
    <div class="pf-label" style="margin-bottom:6px">Ellipsis handling</div>
    <div style="font-size:0.74rem;color:var(--text-faint);line-height:1.5;margin-bottom:8px">
      How to treat <code>"..."</code>, <code>"…"</code>, <code>"‥"</code>, full-width <code>"．．．"</code>, and other multi-dot sequences. <strong>Keep</strong> leaves them alone (TTS may pause briefly). <strong>Period</strong> collapses each to one <code>"."</code> — best for clean sentence rhythm. <strong>Remove</strong> strips entirely (orphan-punct cleanup catches stragglers).
    </div>
    <div id="autoTempoEllipsisGrid" style="display:flex;gap:4px;margin-bottom:14px"></div>
    <div class="pf-label" style="margin-bottom:6px">Character stripping</div>
    <div style="font-size:0.74rem;color:var(--text-faint);line-height:1.5;margin-bottom:8px">
      Click a character below to toggle whether auto-tempo removes it. Characters in the <strong>safe</strong> group can be removed without breaking script structure. Characters in <strong>caution</strong> are used by sentence flow — strip only if your scripts have noisy punctuation.
    </div>
    <div style="font-size:0.7rem;color:var(--text-faint);margin-bottom:4px">Safe to strip (formatting / decorative):</div>
    <div id="autoTempoCharGridSafe" class="auto-tempo-char-grid" style="display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:4px;margin-bottom:10px"></div>
    <div style="font-size:0.7rem;color:var(--text-faint);margin-bottom:4px">Caution (affects sentence rhythm or syntax):</div>
    <div id="autoTempoCharGridCaution" class="auto-tempo-char-grid" style="display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:4px;margin-bottom:10px"></div>
    <div class="sublabel" id="autoTempoStripPreview" style="font-size:0.72rem;margin-top:6px;color:var(--text-dim)"></div>
    <div class="modal-actions" style="margin-top:14px">
      <button class="btn secondary" id="autoTempoOptsResetBtn">reset to defaults</button>
      <button class="btn" id="autoTempoOptsCloseBtn">done</button>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

      <div class="stats-tile" data-chart="hourly"><div class="stats-tile-head"><span class="stats-chart-title">by hour</span><span class="stats-expand-icon">⤢</span></div><canvas id="chartHourly"></canvas></div>
      <div class="stats-tile" data-chart="weekday"><div class="stats-tile-head"><span class="stats-chart-title">by day</span><span class="stats-expand-icon">⤢</span></div><canvas id="chartWeekday"></canvas></div>

      <div class="stats-tile" data-chart="settings"><div class="stats-tile-head"><span class="stats-chart-title">audio + structure</span><span class="stats-expand-icon">⤢</span></div><canvas id="chartSettings"></canvas></div>
      <div class="stats-tile" data-chart="features"><div class="stats-tile-head"><span class="stats-chart-title">features tried</span><span class="stats-expand-icon">⤢</span></div><canvas id="chartFeatures"></canvas></div>
    </div>
    <div class="stats-empty-msg" id="statsEmptyMsg" style="display:none">Complete a few sessions to see your stats here.</div>
  </div>
</div>

<script>
  // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  // HNE DATA BOOTSTRAP — loads the baseline data pack from the Perchance
  // import (advanced-hypnosis-narration-engine-data) and overlays the
  // user's selected language pack (if any) on top. Runs synchronously
  // before any consumer const reads from window.__HNE_DATA__.
  //
  // Failure modes are non-fatal: if root.hneData is missing or throws, the
  // const stubs fall back to [] and the app degrades gracefully.
  // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  (function loadHneData() {
    const FALLBACK = {};
    let baseline = FALLBACK, override = null;
    try {
      const bundleFn = (typeof root !== 'undefined') && root && root.hneData && root.hneData.hneDataBundle;
      if (typeof bundleFn === 'function') baseline = bundleFn() || FALLBACK;
    } catch (e) { console.warn('[HNE_DATA baseline]', e); }
    // User-selected language pack — id stored in localStorage. The actual
    // pack is loaded asynchronously and re-rendered after fetch (see the
    // language-picker handler in Profile > Options). For first paint we
    // use whatever was cached from the previous successful load.
    try {
      const cached = localStorage.getItem('hne.langPackCacheV1');
      if (cached) override = JSON.parse(cached);
    } catch {}
    // Merge: override fills, baseline backfills. Per-key, not per-leaf —
    // a language pack that defines BUILTIN_PERSONAS replaces the whole
    // array (don't half-translate a persona).
    window.__HNE_DATA__ = Object.assign({}, baseline, override || {});
  })();

  // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  // LANGUAGE-PACK PICKER — populates the #pfLanguagePack <select> from
  // root.hneLanguagePacks and wires change → lazy-load → cache → reload.
  // Runs once on DOMContentLoaded so the select exists in the DOM.
  // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function initHneLangPicker() {
    const sel = document.getElementById('pfLanguagePack');
    if (!sel) return;
    const LANG_KEY       = 'hne.langPackIdV1';
    const CACHE_KEY      = 'hne.langPackCacheV1';
    const CACHE_META_KEY = 'hne.langPackCacheMetaV1';

    // Read the language-pack registry. Each entry should be a JSON-parseable
    // string. Perchance lists are accessed via root.<listName> — with the
    // exact accessor depending on Perchance version. Try a few shapes.
    let packs = [];
    try {
      const raw = (typeof root !== 'undefined' && root && root.hneLanguagePacks)
        ? (Array.isArray(root.hneLanguagePacks) ? root.hneLanguagePacks
            : (root.hneLanguagePacks.items || root.hneLanguagePacks.list || []))
        : [];
      // Each entry may already be an object (Perchance-parsed) or a JSON string.
      packs = raw.map(it => {
        if (typeof it === 'object' && it && it.id) return it;
        try { return JSON.parse(String(it)); } catch { return null; }
      }).filter(p => p && p.id && p.slug);
    } catch (e) { console.warn('[langPicker] registry read failed:', e); }
    if (!packs.length) packs = [{ id: 'en', label: 'English (default)', slug: 'advanced-hypnosis-narration-engine-data' }];

    // Populate dropdown
    const currentId = (function () {
      try { return localStorage.getItem(LANG_KEY) || 'en'; } catch { return 'en'; }
    })();
    sel.innerHTML = packs.map(p =>
      `<option value="${p.id.replace(/"/g,'&quot;')}"${p.id === currentId ? ' selected' : ''}>${(p.label || p.id).replace(/[<>&]/g, c => ({'<':'&lt;','>':'&gt;','&':'&amp;'}[c]))}</option>`
    ).join('');

    // Change handler — lazy-load the pack, cache it, reload so every const
    // re-initializes from window.__HNE_DATA__. Reload is the simplest sound
    // strategy: scattered consumers across the script captured array
    // references at parse time, so live-swapping would be fragile.
    sel.addEventListener('change', async (e) => {
      const id = sel.value;
      const pack = packs.find(p => p.id === id);
      if (!pack) return;
      try {
        if (id === 'en' || pack.slug === 'advanced-hypnosis-narration-engine-data') {
          // Default — clear any cached override and reload
          localStorage.removeItem(CACHE_KEY);
          localStorage.removeItem(CACHE_META_KEY);
          localStorage.setItem(LANG_KEY, 'en');
          location.reload();
          return;
        }
        // Non-default — lazy-load via dynamicImport.
        // dynamicImport returns a Promise<{root: {...}, ...}> in Perchance.
        const dyn = (typeof root !== 'undefined' && root && root.dynamicImport);
        if (typeof dyn !== 'function') {
          alert('Language packs need the dynamic-import plugin to load. Falling back to default.');
          sel.value = 'en';
          return;
        }
        const loadingNote = document.createElement('div');
        loadingNote.style.cssText = 'position:fixed;inset:0;background:rgba(0,0,0,0.7);z-index:99999;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;color:#fff;font:14px system-ui';
        loadingNote.textContent = 'Loading language pack…';
        document.body.appendChild(loadingNote);

        const imported = await dyn(pack.slug);
        // The imported generator should expose hneDataBundle() returning an object
        const bundleFn = imported?.hneDataBundle || imported?.root?.hneDataBundle;
        const bundle = (typeof bundleFn === 'function') ? bundleFn() : null;
        if (!bundle || typeof bundle !== 'object') {
          loadingNote.remove();
          alert('Language pack "' + pack.label + '" is malformed (no hneDataBundle). Falling back to default.');
          sel.value = currentId;
          return;
        }
        try {
          localStorage.setItem(CACHE_KEY, JSON.stringify(bundle));
          localStorage.setItem(CACHE_META_KEY, JSON.stringify({ id: pack.id, slug: pack.slug, loadedAt: Date.now() }));
          localStorage.setItem(LANG_KEY, pack.id);
        } catch (storageErr) {
          loadingNote.remove();
          alert('Language pack is too large to cache locally. Falling back to default.');
          sel.value = currentId;
          return;
        }
        location.reload();
      } catch (err) {
        console.warn('[langPicker] load failed:', err);
        alert('Failed to load language pack: ' + (err?.message || err));
        sel.value = currentId;
      }
    });
  });

  // Initialize personal symbol picker
  document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function initSymbolPicker() {
    const symbolInput = $('pfPersonalSymbol');
    const quickPick = $('symbolQuickPick');
    if (!symbolInput || !quickPick) return;

    // Populate quick-pick emoji buttons
    const symbols = ['🧠', '💫', '🌙', '✨', '🔮', '🌿', '🎭', '💎', '🌊', '🔥', '🪷', '⚡'];
    symbols.forEach(sym => {
      const btn = document.createElement('button');
      btn.type = 'button';
      btn.textContent = sym;
      btn.style.cssText = 'padding:4px 8px;border:1px solid var(--border);background:var(--bg);cursor:pointer;border-radius:4px;font-size:1em;transition:all 0.15s';
      btn.onmouseover = () => { btn.style.background = 'var(--bg-hover)'; };
      btn.onmouseout = () => { btn.style.background = 'var(--bg)'; };
      btn.onclick = (e) => {
        e.preventDefault();
        symbolInput.value = sym;
        symbolInput.dispatchEvent(new Event('change', { bubbles: true }));
        symbolInput.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true }));
      };
      quickPick.appendChild(btn);
    });

    // Update sidebar avatar in real-time when input changes
    symbolInput.addEventListener('input', () => {
      const avatar = $('sidebarAvatar');
      if (avatar) avatar.textContent = symbolInput.value || '🧠';
    });

    symbolInput.addEventListener('change', () => {
      saveProfile();
    });
  });
</script>
<script>

  const IDB_NAME = 'stillness-db';
  const IDB_VER  = 1;
  const IDB_STORE = 'kv';
  let _idb = null;
  let _idbOpenP = null;

  function _openIdb() {
    if (_idb) return Promise.resolve(_idb);
    if (_idbOpenP) return _idbOpenP;
    _idbOpenP = new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
      const req = indexedDB.open(IDB_NAME, IDB_VER);
      req.onupgradeneeded = e => {
        const db = e.target.result;
        if (!db.objectStoreNames.contains(IDB_STORE)) db.createObjectStore(IDB_STORE);
      };
      req.onsuccess = e => {
        _idb = e.target.result;
        _idb.onclose = () => { _idb = null; _idbOpenP = null; };
        resolve(_idb);
      };
      req.onerror = e => { _idbOpenP = null; reject(e.target.error ?? new Error('IDB open failed')); };
    });
    return _idbOpenP;
  }
  async function idbGet(key) {
    try {
      const db = await _openIdb();
      return await new Promise((res, rej) => {
        const r = db.transaction(IDB_STORE, 'readonly').objectStore(IDB_STORE).get(key);
        r.onsuccess = () => res(r.result ?? null);
        r.onerror = e => rej(e.target.error);
      });
    } catch { return null; }
  }
  async function idbSet(key, value) {
    try {
      const db = await _openIdb();
      await new Promise((res, rej) => {
        const tx = db.transaction(IDB_STORE, 'readwrite');
        tx.objectStore(IDB_STORE).put(value, key);
        tx.oncomplete = res;
        tx.onerror = e => rej(e.target.error);
      });
    } catch (e) {

      console.warn(`[idbSet] failed for "${key}":`, e?.message || e);
    }
  }
  async function idbDel(key) {
    try {
      const db = await _openIdb();
      await new Promise((res, rej) => {
        const tx = db.transaction(IDB_STORE, 'readwrite');
        tx.objectStore(IDB_STORE).delete(key);
        tx.oncomplete = res;
        tx.onerror = e => rej(e.target.error);
      });
    } catch (e) {
      console.warn(`[idbDel] failed for "${key}":`, e?.message || e);
    }
  }

  const TOAST_ICONS    = { info: 'ℹ', success: '✓', warn: '⚠', error: '✕' };
  const TOAST_DURATION = { info: 3500, success: 3000, warn: 5000, error: 0 };

  function toast(type, message, ms) {
    const container = $('toastContainer');
    if (!container) return;
    const duration = ms !== undefined ? ms : TOAST_DURATION[type] || 3500;
    const el = document.createElement('div');
    el.className = `toast toast-${type}`;
    el.innerHTML = `<span class="toast-icon">${TOAST_ICONS[type] || 'ℹ'}</span><span class="toast-text">${escapeHtml(message)}</span><button class="toast-close">×</button>`;
    el.querySelector('.toast-close').addEventListener('click', () => dismissToast(el));
    if (duration === 0) el.addEventListener('click', () => dismissToast(el));
    container.appendChild(el);
    requestAnimationFrame(() => el.classList.add('show'));
    let timer = null;
    if (duration > 0) timer = setTimeout(() => dismissToast(el), duration);
    function dismissToast(t) { clearTimeout(timer); t.classList.remove('show'); setTimeout(() => t.remove(), 220); }
    return el;
  }

  function _isInSessionPlayback() {
    try { return document.body.classList.contains('in-session') && !state._allowToastsInSession; }
    catch { return false; }
  }
  const notify = {
    info:    (msg, ms) => { if (state._preGenMode || _isInSessionPlayback()) return null; return toast('info', msg, ms); },
    success: (msg, ms) => { if (state._preGenMode || _isInSessionPlayback()) return null; return toast('success', msg, ms); },
    warn:    (msg, ms) => { if (state._preGenMode || _isInSessionPlayback()) return null; return toast('warn', msg, ms); },

    error:   (msg, ms) => { if (state._preGenMode) { console.warn('[pre-gen suppressed error]', msg); return null; } return toast('error', msg, ms); },
  };

  function confirmAsync(message, opts = {}) {
    return new Promise(resolve => {
      const backdrop = document.createElement('div');
      backdrop.className = 'modal-backdrop visible';
      backdrop.innerHTML = `<div class="modal" style="max-width:420px">
        <div style="font-size:0.88rem;line-height:1.55;color:var(--text-dim);white-space:pre-line">${escapeHtml(message)}</div>
        <div class="modal-actions" style="margin-top:16px">
          <button class="btn secondary" id="_confirmNo">${opts.cancelText || 'cancel'}</button>
          <button class="btn" id="_confirmYes">${opts.okText || 'confirm'}</button>
        </div>
      </div>`;
      document.body.appendChild(backdrop);
      const cleanup = (val) => { backdrop.remove(); resolve(val); };
      backdrop.querySelector('#_confirmYes').addEventListener('click', () => cleanup(true));
      backdrop.querySelector('#_confirmNo').addEventListener('click', () => cleanup(false));
      backdrop.addEventListener('click', e => { if (e.target === backdrop) cleanup(false); });
    });
  }

  function alertModal(title, message) {
    return new Promise(resolve => {
      const backdrop = document.createElement('div');
      backdrop.className = 'modal-backdrop visible';
      backdrop.innerHTML = `<div class="modal" style="max-width:420px">
        ${title ? `<h2 style="margin:0 0 12px">${escapeHtml(title)}</h2>` : ''}
        <div style="font-size:0.84rem;line-height:1.55;color:var(--text-dim);white-space:pre-line">${escapeHtml(message)}</div>
        <div class="modal-actions" style="margin-top:16px">
          <button class="btn" id="_alertOk">ok</button>
        </div>
      </div>`;
      document.body.appendChild(backdrop);
      const cleanup = () => { backdrop.remove(); resolve(); };
      backdrop.querySelector('#_alertOk').addEventListener('click', cleanup);
      backdrop.addEventListener('click', e => { if (e.target === backdrop) cleanup(); });
    });
  }

  function promptAsync(message, opts = {}) {
    return new Promise(resolve => {
      const backdrop = document.createElement('div');
      backdrop.className = 'modal-backdrop visible';
      const ml = Number.isFinite(opts.maxLength) ? opts.maxLength : 80;
      backdrop.innerHTML = `<div class="modal" style="max-width:440px">
        ${opts.title ? `<h2 style="margin:0 0 10px">${escapeHtml(opts.title)}</h2>` : ''}
        <div style="font-size:0.86rem;line-height:1.55;color:var(--text-dim);white-space:pre-line;margin-bottom:10px">${escapeHtml(message)}</div>
        <input type="text" id="_promptInput" maxlength="${ml}" placeholder="${escapeHtml(opts.placeholder || '')}" value="${escapeHtml(opts.initial || '')}" style="width:100%;padding:10px;background:var(--bg-deep);border:1px solid var(--glass-border);color:var(--text);font-size:0.95rem">
        <div class="modal-actions" style="margin-top:16px">
          <button class="btn secondary" id="_promptCancel">${escapeHtml(opts.cancelText || 'cancel')}</button>
          <button class="btn" id="_promptOk">${escapeHtml(opts.okText || 'ok')}</button>
        </div>
      </div>`;
      document.body.appendChild(backdrop);
      const input = backdrop.querySelector('#_promptInput');
      const cleanup = (val) => { backdrop.remove(); resolve(val); };
      const submit = () => {
        const v = (input.value || '').trim();
        cleanup(v || null);
      };
      backdrop.querySelector('#_promptOk').addEventListener('click', submit);
      backdrop.querySelector('#_promptCancel').addEventListener('click', () => cleanup(null));
      backdrop.addEventListener('click', e => { if (e.target === backdrop) cleanup(null); });
      input.addEventListener('keydown', e => {
        if (e.key === 'Enter')  { e.preventDefault(); submit(); }
        if (e.key === 'Escape') { e.preventDefault(); cleanup(null); }
      });

      setTimeout(() => input.focus(), 0);
    });
  }

  try {
    const isTouchScreen = window.matchMedia("(pointer: coarse)").matches;
    const isSafari = navigator.vendor?.indexOf('Apple') > -1
      && !navigator.userAgent.includes('CriOS')
      && !navigator.userAgent.includes('FxiOS');
    if (isSafari && window.innerWidth < 800 && isTouchScreen) {
      const viewportMeta = document.querySelector("[name=viewport]");
      if (viewportMeta && !viewportMeta.getAttribute("content").includes("maximum-scale")) {
        viewportMeta.setAttribute("content",
          viewportMeta.getAttribute("content") + ", maximum-scale=1");
      }
    }
  } catch(e) { console.error(e); }

  const HNE_SCRIPT_LIBRARY = (() => { try { const el = document.getElementById('hne-script-library-data'); return el ? JSON.parse(el.textContent) : null; } catch(e) { console.warn('[HNE_SCRIPT_LIBRARY load]', e); return null; } })();

  const METHODS = (window.__HNE_DATA__ && window.__HNE_DATA__.METHODS) || [];

  const HNE_CUSTOM_METHODS_KEY = 'hne_custom_methods_v1';
  const VALID_METHOD_CATEGORIES = ['therapeutic', 'demonstration', 'advanced'];

  function loadCustomMethods() {
    try {
      const raw = localStorage.getItem(HNE_CUSTOM_METHODS_KEY);
      if (!raw) return [];
      const arr = JSON.parse(raw);
      if (!Array.isArray(arr)) return [];
      return arr.filter(m => m && typeof m === 'object' && typeof m.id === 'string' && typeof m.name === 'string');
    } catch { return []; }
  }
  function saveCustomMethods(arr) {
    try { localStorage.setItem(HNE_CUSTOM_METHODS_KEY, JSON.stringify(arr || [])); }
    catch (e) { notify.warn?.('Could not save methods: ' + (e.message || 'storage full')); }
  }

  function _normalizeCustomMethod(raw, existingIds = new Set()) {
    const baseRaw = String(raw?.id || raw?.name || 'method').toLowerCase().trim();
    const base = baseRaw.startsWith('u_') ? baseRaw : 'u_' + baseRaw.replace(/[^a-z0-9_-]+/g, '_').slice(0, 28);
    let id = base, suffix = 1;
    const builtinIds = new Set(METHODS.filter(m => !m._custom).map(m => m.id));
    while (existingIds.has(id) || builtinIds.has(id)) id = base + '-' + (++suffix);
    const cat = VALID_METHOD_CATEGORIES.includes(raw?.category) ? raw.category : 'therapeutic';
    const tags = Array.isArray(raw?.tags)
      ? raw.tags.map(t => String(t).slice(0, 30)).filter(Boolean).slice(0, 8)
      : [];
    return {
      id,
      name:    String(raw?.name || 'Untitled Method').slice(0, 60),
      tagline: String(raw?.tagline || '').slice(0, 200),
      tags,
      category: cat,
      detail:  String(raw?.detail || '').slice(0, 4000),
      visual:  String(raw?.visual || 'colorwash').slice(0, 30),
      drone:   !!raw?.drone,
      pacing:  String(raw?.pacing || 'permissive').slice(0, 30),
      _custom: true,
      _created: Number(raw?._created) || Date.now(),
    };
  }

  function _mergeCustomMethods() {
    if (!Array.isArray(METHODS)) return;

    for (let i = METHODS.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
      if (METHODS[i]?._custom) METHODS.splice(i, 1);
    }
    for (const cm of loadCustomMethods()) {

      METHODS.push({ ...cm });
    }
  }

  function _refreshCustomMethods() {
    _mergeCustomMethods();
    if (typeof renderMethodGrid === 'function') renderMethodGrid();
    if (typeof renderMethodDetail === 'function') renderMethodDetail();
    const list = $('customMethodsList');
    if (list) _renderCustomMethodsList();
  }

  const PACING_RULES = {
    monotony:       `3-6 word phrases. Repeat a small set of core phrases. No storytelling. Leave a long, deliberate pause after each phrase — write each one on its own short line. Boring is the mechanism.`,
    structured:     `Follow method structure exactly. Each instruction stands alone on its own line — let it breathe before the next. Longer phrases (8-15 words) for clarity.`,
    narrative:      `Vivid sensory imagery. Phrases 8-15 words. Let sentences breathe — short paragraphs separated by line breaks. Use ellipses (...) when an image needs a moment to land.`,
    focused:        `All phrases reference the focal sensation. Build slowly with deliberate pauses between observations. For demonstration methods: direct suggestions ("your hand IS", "you CANNOT") once state is established.`,
    confusion:      `Long sentences with embedded clauses, paradoxes. Occasional brief clarity. Pauses come naturally from sentence weight — let complex sentences end fully before the next begins.`,
    fractionation:  `Match cycle pattern. Mark transitions: "And opening!" / "And closing again..." Each induction deeper.`,
    'eye-roll':     `Precise Spiegel sequence. Each step on its own short line so it has time to land. After eye closure: breath/floating language.`,
    breath:         `Voice IS metronome. Count clearly. Real timing 4 in, 7 hold, 8 out.`,
    'body-scan':    `Head-to-toe order. Name, notice, release each part. Phrases 5-10 words. Each body part gets its own line; let attention move slowly between them.`,
    permissive:     `"Perhaps", "you might notice", "in your own time". Phrases 5-12 words. Use "..." within sentences to mark gentle micro-pauses; paragraph breaks for longer settling.`,
    rapid:          `Short, commanding phrases. 2-5 words. No hesitation. Speed IS the mechanism. Build speed, then sudden "SLEEP" / "DROP" / "NOW" followed by a long deliberate silence — write a paragraph break and a single ellipsis (…) on its own line to mark it.`,
    elman:          `Step-by-step clinical protocol. Test each step before proceeding. "Close your eyes. Now relax them so completely that they just won't work. Test them. Good." One step per line, each fully complete before the next. Confident, professional, never rushing but never lingering.`,
  };

  const INTENTION_TYPES = (window.__HNE_DATA__ && window.__HNE_DATA__.INTENTION_TYPES) || [];

  const HNE_CUSTOM_INTENTIONS_KEY = 'hne_custom_intentions_v1';

  function loadCustomIntentions() {
    try {
      const raw = localStorage.getItem(HNE_CUSTOM_INTENTIONS_KEY);
      if (!raw) return [];
      const arr = JSON.parse(raw);
      if (!Array.isArray(arr)) return [];
      return arr.filter(i => i && typeof i === 'object' && typeof i.id === 'string' && typeof i.label === 'string');
    } catch { return []; }
  }
  function saveCustomIntentions(arr) {
    try { localStorage.setItem(HNE_CUSTOM_INTENTIONS_KEY, JSON.stringify(arr || [])); }
    catch (e) { notify.warn?.('Could not save intentions: ' + (e.message || 'storage full')); }
  }

  function _normalizeCustomIntention(raw, existingIds = new Set()) {
    const baseRaw = String(raw?.id || raw?.label || 'custom').toLowerCase().trim();
    const base = baseRaw.startsWith('u_') ? baseRaw : 'u_' + baseRaw.replace(/[^a-z0-9_-]+/g, '_').slice(0, 28);
    let id = base, suffix = 1;
    while (existingIds.has(id) || (typeof INTENTION_TYPES !== 'undefined' && id in INTENTION_TYPES && id !== raw?.id)) {
      id = base + '-' + (++suffix);
    }
    return {
      id,
      label:    String(raw?.label || 'Untitled').slice(0, 80),
      hint:     String(raw?.hint || '').slice(0, 200),
      guidance: String(raw?.guidance || '').slice(0, 6000),
      group:    typeof raw?.group === 'string' ? raw.group.slice(0, 30) : 'Custom',
      _custom:  true,
      _created: Number(raw?._created) || Date.now(),
    };
  }

  function _mergeCustomIntentions() {
    if (typeof INTENTION_TYPES === 'undefined') return;

    for (const k of Object.keys(INTENTION_TYPES)) {
      if (INTENTION_TYPES[k]?._custom) delete INTENTION_TYPES[k];
    }
    for (const ci of loadCustomIntentions()) {
      INTENTION_TYPES[ci.id] = {
        label:    ci.label,
        hint:     ci.hint,
        guidance: ci.guidance,
        _custom:  true,
        _group:   ci.group || 'Custom',
      };
    }

    if (typeof POST_SESSION_REFLECTIONS !== 'undefined') {
      for (const k of Object.keys(POST_SESSION_REFLECTIONS)) {
        if (k.startsWith('u_') && !INTENTION_TYPES[k]) delete POST_SESSION_REFLECTIONS[k];
      }
      for (const ci of loadCustomIntentions()) {
        POST_SESSION_REFLECTIONS[ci.id] ||= 'Notice what shifts in the next 24 hours. The intention is already at work.';
      }
    }
  }

  function _renderIntentionDropdown() {
    const sel = $('intentionType');
    if (!sel) return;
    const current = sel.value;

    const BUILTIN_GROUPS = [
      ['Foundation', ['emotional','trigger','behavior','release','perception','anchor','trance']],
      ['Performance', ['performance','focus','creativity']],
      ['Body', ['pain','sleep','energy']],
      ['Demonstration', ['convincer','novelty']],
      ['Advanced', ['time','amnesia','identity','surrender','conditioning']],
    ];
    const seen = new Set();
    let html = '';
    for (const [groupLabel, ids] of BUILTIN_GROUPS) {
      let groupHtml = '';
      for (const id of ids) {
        if (INTENTION_TYPES[id]) {
          const lbl = INTENTION_TYPES[id].label || id;
          groupHtml += `<option value="${escapeHtml(id)}">${escapeHtml(lbl)}</option>`;
          seen.add(id);
        }
      }
      if (groupHtml) html += `<optgroup label="${escapeHtml(groupLabel)}">${groupHtml}</optgroup>`;
    }

    const customs = Object.entries(INTENTION_TYPES)
      .filter(([id, v]) => v?._custom && !seen.has(id))
      .sort((a, b) => (a[1].label || '').localeCompare(b[1].label || ''));
    if (customs.length) {

      const byGroup = {};
      for (const [id, v] of customs) {
        const g = v._group || 'Custom';
        (byGroup[g] ||= []).push([id, v]);
      }
      for (const [g, items] of Object.entries(byGroup)) {
        let inner = '';
        for (const [id, v] of items) inner += `<option value="${escapeHtml(id)}">${escapeHtml(v.label)}</option>`;
        html += `<optgroup label="${escapeHtml(g + ' (yours)')}">${inner}</optgroup>`;
      }
    }
    sel.innerHTML = html;

    if (current && INTENTION_TYPES[current]) sel.value = current;
    else sel.value = 'trigger';
    state.intentionType = sel.value;
    if (typeof updateIntentionHint === 'function') updateIntentionHint();
  }

  function _refreshCustomIntentions() {
    _mergeCustomIntentions();
    _renderIntentionDropdown();
    const list = $('customIntentionsList');
    if (list) _renderCustomIntentionsList();
  }

  const POST_SESSION_REFLECTION_FALLBACK = 'Take what was useful. Let the rest settle on its own.';

  const BUILTIN_PERSONAS = (window.__HNE_DATA__ && window.__HNE_DATA__.BUILTIN_PERSONAS) || [];

  function _syncDebugState() {
    const d = state._debug;
    const on = !!document.getElementById('debugMode')?.checked;
    d.skipRescue       = on;
    d.skipCooldown     = on;
    d.skipSafetyGate   = on;
    d.skipMethodGating = on;
    d.verbose          = on;
    d.showAdult        = on;
    _renderDebugBanner();

    try {
      if (typeof _renderSessionModeDropdown === 'function') _renderSessionModeDropdown();
      if (typeof _renderContentPacksGrid === 'function') _renderContentPacksGrid();
      if (typeof _refreshAdultFilterVisibility === 'function') _refreshAdultFilterVisibility();
      if (typeof renderPersonaGrid === 'function') renderPersonaGrid();
      if (typeof renderMethodGrid  === 'function') renderMethodGrid();
      if (typeof renderPresets     === 'function') renderPresets();
      if (typeof renderPrograms    === 'function') renderPrograms();
    } catch {}
  }

  function _renderDebugBanner() {
    const anyOn = Object.values(state._debug).some(Boolean);
    let el = document.getElementById('_debugBanner');
    if (anyOn && !el) {
      el = document.createElement('div');
      el.id = '_debugBanner';
      el.textContent = '\u26A0 DEBUG';
      el.style.cssText = 'position:fixed;top:8px;right:8px;z-index:99999;background:rgba(220,60,60,0.92);color:#fff;font-size:0.62rem;font-weight:600;letter-spacing:0.08em;padding:4px 8px;border-radius:4px;pointer-events:none;box-shadow:0 2px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.3);';
      document.body.appendChild(el);
    } else if (!anyOn && el) {
      el.remove();
    }
  }

  function sdLog(...args) {
    if (state._debug?.verbose) console.log('[SmartDirector]', ...args);
  }

  const DIRECTOR_AUDIT_KEY = 'hne_director_audit_v1';
  const DIRECTOR_AUDIT_CAP = 200;
  let _directorAuditCache = null;

  async function _loadDirectorAudit() {
    if (Array.isArray(_directorAuditCache)) return _directorAuditCache;
    try {
      const raw = await idbGet(DIRECTOR_AUDIT_KEY);
      _directorAuditCache = Array.isArray(raw) ? raw : [];
    } catch { _directorAuditCache = []; }
    return _directorAuditCache;
  }
  async function _saveDirectorAudit() {
    if (!Array.isArray(_directorAuditCache)) return;
    try { await idbSet(DIRECTOR_AUDIT_KEY, _directorAuditCache); } catch {}
  }

  async function recordDirectorAudit(kind, payload) {
    if (state.smartDirectorEnabled === false) return;
    try {
      const log = await _loadDirectorAudit();
      log.unshift({ ts: Date.now(), kind, ...(payload || {}) });
      if (log.length > DIRECTOR_AUDIT_CAP) log.length = DIRECTOR_AUDIT_CAP;
      await _saveDirectorAudit();

      if (typeof renderDirectorAuditPanel === 'function') {
        try { renderDirectorAuditPanel(); } catch {}
      }
    } catch (e) { console.debug('[director audit] record failed:', e); }
  }

  function _summarizeAdaptive(adaptive) {
    if (!adaptive) return null;
    const top = (map, n = 3) => {
      const entries = Object.entries(map || {})
        .map(([k, v]) => [k, Math.round(Number(v) * 100) / 100])
        .sort((a, b) => Math.abs(b[1]) - Math.abs(a[1]))
        .slice(0, n);
      return Object.fromEntries(entries);
    };
    return {
      ts: adaptive.ts || 0,
      difficulty: typeof adaptive.difficulty === 'number' ? Math.round(adaptive.difficulty * 100) / 100 : null,
      recoveryMode: !!adaptive.recoveryMode,
      topPersona: top(adaptive.w?.p),
      topMethod:  top(adaptive.w?.m),
      topSound:   top(adaptive.w?.s),
      topLength:  top(adaptive.w?.ln),
    };
  }

  const SHARED_LINKS_KEY = 'hne_shared_links_v1';
  const SHARED_LINKS_CAP = 100;
  let _sharedLinksCache = null;

  async function _loadSharedLinks() {
    if (Array.isArray(_sharedLinksCache)) return _sharedLinksCache;
    try {
      const raw = await idbGet(SHARED_LINKS_KEY);
      _sharedLinksCache = Array.isArray(raw) ? raw : [];
    } catch { _sharedLinksCache = []; }
    return _sharedLinksCache;
  }
  async function _saveSharedLinks() {
    if (!Array.isArray(_sharedLinksCache)) return;
    try { await idbSet(SHARED_LINKS_KEY, _sharedLinksCache); } catch {}
  }

  async function _recordSharedLink(meta) {
    if (!meta || !meta.url) return;
    const log = await _loadSharedLinks();
    log.unshift({
      id: 'sh_' + Date.now().toString(36) + Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 6),
      ...meta,
      revoked: false,
      revokedAt: null,
    });
    if (log.length > SHARED_LINKS_CAP) log.length = SHARED_LINKS_CAP;
